items.title: Australia is a global battery giant and one state is leading the charge

children.content: Western Australia in particular is proving to the world that grid-scale batteries are no longer a complementary technology but can play a central role in power grids.

items.title: Battery boom marks a new political chapter in Australia's energy wars

children.content: Within the government, there's quiet confidence the battery boom will continue to gather pace and help shield Australia from both global shocks and domestic demand surges, driving prices even lower.

image.altText: Chris Bowen at a pressconference, gesturing with his hand. He wears a suit

avatar.alt: David Speers sits on a couch in front of a yellow screen, smiling at the camera, in a suit and open-necked shirt.

items.title: Iran's internet is coming back online. But it's not back to normal

children.content: Reports of an increase in internet traffic are filtering through, but experts say Iran has a long way to go to get back up to speed.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a man's hands typing on a keyboard in a dark room.

items.title: Israel tells all residents in southern Lebanon to leave

children.content: It is the first time Israel has made such a sweeping demand since US President Donald Trump announced in April a ceasefire had been imposed in Lebanon.

image.altText: A convoy of military vehicles driving down a dirt road in a grassy plain.

items.title: How Australia led the charge for the world to ban social media for kids

children.content: The UK could become the latest nation to implement a social media ban, after British Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer vowed to introduce a "game changer" policy.

image.altText: Keir Starmer in a dark shirt with his sleeves rolled up, siting while smiling next to two children with red hats.

items.title: Young saviour and an answered prayer deliver Blues incredible Origin win

children.content: When the Blues needed heroes to get on top of the Maroons in State of Origin I, they turned to a debutante and the oldest player on the field.

articles.title: Kalyn Ponga's Origin send-off leaves Queensland legend 'in shock'

children.content: Maroons great Darren Lockyer says the decision to send off Kalyn Ponga late in State of Origin will be hard to accept for Queenslanders.

image.altText: Kalyn Ponga hits Tolu Koula during State of Origin.

articles.title: Quick hits: Blues' selection gamble pays off, bunker sparks controversy

children.content: The Blues leave it late, the Maroons finish with 12 players, while the bunker draws the ire of players, commentators and spectators alike. Here are five quick hits from State of Origin I.

image.altText: A composite image of James Tedesco, Kalyn Ponga and Cameron Murray.

gallery.altText: Ethan Strange celebrates a Blues try in State of Origin I.

thumbnail.altText: Ethan Strange celebrates a Blues try in State of Origin I.

items.title: ISIS-linked woman barred from Australia tried to board flight

children.content: An Australian woman with links to ISIS, who is barred from Australia on a temporary exclusion order, tried to board a flight home with the other members of Tuesday's returning cohort, a Senate estimates hearing has been told.

image.altText: A Qatar plane on the tarmac at Melbourne international airport.

items.title: Trump says US 'not satisfied' with Iran peace deal proposals

children.content: It comes as the White House rejects a draft deal being reported by Iranian state TV as a "fabrication".

image.altText: An oil tanker docked with multiple vessels around it next to a crude oil terminal.

items.title: Video shows divers finding five Laos cavers alive, two remain missing

children.content: A rescue mission has been underway for more than a week to find seven people who entered a cave in central Laos before becoming trapped in a landslide triggered by heavy rain.

gallery.altText: A shadowy image shows people wearing head torches smiling.
Three men in helmets stand at the muddy entrance to a small cave.

thumbnail.altText: A shadowy image shows people wearing head torches smiling.
Three men in helmets stand at the muddy entrance to a small cave.

items.title: Fears Melbourne man met with foul play on trip to India

children.content: The family of maths teacher Sunil Sharma say the 66-year-old disappeared nearly a week ago, while trying to sell a property in the Punjabi city of Amritsar.

image.altText: Surbhi and Sunil Sharma smile, taking a selfie together in a city.

items.title: Kalshi and Polymarket are billion dollar bets banned from Australia — for now

children.content: Kalshi and Polymarket have gone from obscure start-ups to two of the world's biggest prediction markets where users bet on yes-or-no questions spanning politics, pop culture and war.

image.altText: A man speaking on stage at a lecturn.

items.title: UK spy chief says 500,000 Russians killed in Ukraine war so far

children.content: The head of a major UK intelligence agency is warning that Moscow and other adversaries could use artificial intelligence and other emerging technology to conduct "hybrid warfare" against western allies.

image.altText: A man stands in front of the Russian flag.

items.title: FIFA faces investigation over 'impossibly high' World Cup ticket prices

children.content: In a joint statement, New Jersey's attorney-general accused the football governing body of having turned "buying a ticket to the World Cup into a gauntlet of confusion, fake scarcity, and impossibly high prices".

image.altText: The FIFA World Cup 2026 logo on the side of a stadium roof.

items.title: Why this empty room has the internet terrified

children.content: Forget about slashers and body horror — the next evolution of fright stems from an fluorescent-lit room.

items.title: Pop art-inspired willy wagtails taking flight beyond Surf Coast

children.content: A Victorian artist paints hundreds of willie wagtails a week to give away locally and now they're popping up all over the world.

image.altText: Mike McLean paints his willie wagtails on pieces of reclaimed timber.

items.title: Quiz: Can you identify Aussie TV shows from a single frame?

children.content: How well do you know your Neighbours from your Deadloch? Take our single-frame Australian TV quiz to find out.

image.altText: Show artwork for Kath & Kim, featuring Kath and Kim standing side by side in bold outfits and hats, holding glasses of wine

items.title: PM response to truth-telling process 'hugely significant'

children.content: The Walk for Truth ended at Parliament House today and while there was not a firm commitment from the PM, many felt the response was a 'positive move'.

items.title: Labor MP defends electoral registration to empty block amid rebuild

children.content: Dickson MP Ali France has been referred to the Australian Electoral Commission with claims she is registered to vote at a vacant suburban block in her north Brisbane electorate.

image.altText: A woman sits in parliament with a maroon scarf

items.title: What we know, and what we don't, about new changes to Centrelink system

children.content: The federal government has proposed changes to Australia's $2 billion employment services system to make them “more responsive, effective and dignified”.

image.altText: Centrelink sign.

items.title: This 24yo started gambling as a teenager and has now lost 900k

children.content: From footy tips to Taylor Swift bets, teenagers are being lured into gambling. Almost 34 per cent of Australian teenagers gamble and everyone from teachers, experts, to the young people themselves are sounding the alarm.

items.title: Bondi shooting victim labelled 'subhuman' in online abuse of inquiry witnesses

children.content: Victims of antisemitism have been targeted with death threats and depicted as animals in AI-generated images after giving evidence to the royal commission examining the Bondi terror attack.

image.altText: NSW Police at royal commission hearing

items.title: Chalmers flags 'legitimate' small business CGT debate

children.content: The government is treading carefully on carve-outs to its capital gains tax legislation amid backlash to its plan.

image.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers in a navy suit and maroon tie, pressing his lips tightly.

items.title: Bulk carrier rescues two fishermen and Smokey the dog after boat sinks

children.content: An emergency distress beacon activation off the North Queensland coast yesterday afternoon sparked a coordinated rescue involving a bulk carrier and rescue jet.

A group of people and a dog on the deck of a bulk carrier.

items.title: Multiple crime scenes declared after man dies in Childers area

children.content: One man is dead and two others are in hospital, as police declare multiple crime scenes in a regional Queensland town.

image.altText: Men in blue uniforms standing on a road.

items.title: Russia looks for new soldiers in Transnistria as war casualty numbers grow

children.content: The Kremlin is trying to recruit more soldiers for its war in Ukraine but an analyst says at the rate of casualties, "Russia cannot replace recruits fast enough to keep the 'meat grinder' fed."

image.altText: Russian President Vladimir Putin  reaches his hand across a table

items.title: Police say cause of Kumanjayi White's death in custody still not known

children.content: The Northern Territory police commissioner says the cause of 24-year-old Kumanjayi White's death in custody remains undetermined, with the criminal case against the officers who restrained him now closed.

image.altText: A woman bends over in mourning next to a pile of flowers laid on the ground.

items.title: Why Blak Douglas won't enter the Archibald Prize again

children.content: The acclaimed Dunghutti artist is using his latest exhibition to challenge perceptions of Aboriginal art and the systemic issues creatives face in Australia.

image.altText: Artist Blak Douglas, born Adam Douglas Hill, has opened a new exhibition at NSW Parliament House entitled 'Home Flown'.

items.title: Record-breaking rain welcomed in drought-hit parts of NSW

children.content: Up to 100 millimetres of rain has been welcomed across drought-hit parts of western NSW, but some farmers expecting relief were left disappointed.

image.altText: Wet paddocks through the window of a ute driving through the landscape

gallery.altText: Steady rain over brown paddocks with water starting to pool.

thumbnail.altText: Steady rain over brown paddocks with water starting to pool.

items.title: Inquest hears NDIS provider delayed suicide threat reports before teen's death

children.content: The inquest in Southport is examining whether any deficiencies in the boy's care or accommodation caused or contributed to his death.

image.altText: portrait of teen

items.title: Rodent faeces, animal blood, mould spark $60k penalty for country bakery

children.content: Inspectors found animal blood dripping onto bread, rodent faeces on food and spreading mould at the Albany French Hot Bread Shop, triggering prosecution and $60,000 in penalties for the owners.

image.altText: A wide of a shop front

items.title: Bystanders foil alleged kidnap attempt of woman changing tyre in Sydney

children.content: A 27-year-old woman was changing a tyre when a man allegedly threatened her with a knife and attempted to drag her into an SUV, NSW Police said.

image.altText: A man in a tracksuit is handcuffed while surrounded by several NSW Police officers.

items.title: Council buys land for $1, but what's the catch?

children.content: A Midwest city council buys Crown land for $1 to develop more than 100 dwellings to house key workers, but WA's shadow local government minister says it is an example of more responsibility being placed on councils.

image.altText: A drone image of a block of land with bushland on surrounded by buildings, road and the beach.

items.title: No 'plan B' for submarines if AUKUS fails, says defence minister

children.content: The defence minister brushes off calls for a "plan B" in case AUKUS fails, declaring it would be a step towards "giving up" on acquiring any new submarines at all.

gallery.altText: A submarine moves through the water causing a massive wake behind it, some crew visible above deck.

thumbnail.altText: A submarine moves through the water causing a massive wake behind it, some crew visible above deck.
